'''Mike Bennett''' is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Bennett played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] as a {{rlp|sr}} forward, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Gordos|first=Phil|title=Long breaks Bulls' hearts|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2339919.stm|publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=19 November 2013|date=19 October 2002}}</ref> St Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Bennett played from the interchange bench in Saints' 26–4 victory. In 2005, Bennett represented [[England national rugby league team|England]], coming off the bench in a 22–12 victory against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=France serve notice of intent, but England redress balance|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/oct/24/rugbyleague.englandrugbyleagueteam|work=The Guardian|accessdate=19 November 2013|date=24 October 2005}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title='I felt like a pawn' – Higham|url=http://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/news/i-felt-like-a-pawn-higham-1-732487|work=St Helens Reporter|publisher=Johnston Press|accessdate=19 November 2013|date=28 October 2005}}</ref> As 2006 Super League champions, St. Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Bennett played as a {{rlp|sr}} in the Saints' 18–14 victory.

Bennett announced on 1 September 2008 that he would retire at the end of the 2008 season due to injury.<ref>{{cite web|title=Injury forces Bennett to retire|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/st_helens/7592445.stm|publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=19 November 2013|date=1 September 2008}}</ref>
